structured.title: Family Breakfast Chat About Money And Football Kits
structured.overview: Over breakfast, the family chats casually about food, clothes, money, and football kits. An adult (likely a parent) jokes about the price of an extra hash brown and recommends the cheesy bacon flatbread while speaking partly in Dutch and English. They discuss the children’s tops and kit sizes, then the parent offers that if either child wants a new orange, blue, or green kit, they can get two for £28 and mummy and daddy will pay half, effectively making it £7 each. The kids hesitate, and Ramsey shows mild interest but not a firm decision. The conversation shifts to missing cash and cards, with the parent explaining how spending on a card doesn’t feel like real money and reminding Ramsey that some of the money has already been spent (possibly on wrestling tickets). They talk briefly about uncomfortable eggshell in food, having jackets and baseball caps in the rucksack in case it’s sunny, and Ramsey’s hairstyle, including wanting a "modern mullet" similar to someone with long hair. The chat ends playfully with references to people named Sam and Ben and light teasing about preferences.

apps_results.content:
## Breakfast and small talk  
- Mention of hash browns costing about £1.79  
- Comment that it's expensive but very tasty  
- Light chat about cheesy bacon flatbread being “the best”  

## Football kits and money offer  
- Offer to buy orange, blue, or green kit  
- Deal: 2 shirts for £28, so £14 each  
- Parents will pay half of each shirt  

## Money, cards, and spending habits  
- Discussion about missing cash and £10 notes  
- Reminder that card spending “doesn’t feel like real money”  
- Mention of a £50 note, likely spent on tickets  

## Clothes, caps, and weather  
- Baseball caps and possibly jackets in the rucksack  
- Caps suggested to keep sun out of eyes  
- Preference not to care how the cap looks, just function  

## Haircuts and hairstyle ideas  
- Talk about needing another haircut  
- Interest in a “modern mullet” style  
- Comparison to someone with long hair and shaved areas  

## Names and references  
- Names mentioned: Jude, Judith, Sebastian, Margaret, Sam, Ben  
- Reference to tickets for WWE  
- Brief mention of Canada in passing  

## Next steps  
- Decide whether to get the discounted football shirts  
- Use caps/jackets if it gets sunny  
- Plan and get the desired modern mullet haircut
